examples: Classification: Identifying an email as spam or not spam.
memory_hooks: Prediction = Prices (Numbers). Classification = Categories (Classes). Generative = Genesis (Creation).

notes.topic_title: Identify guiding principles for responsible AI
notes.summary: Microsoft defines six core principles to ensure AI is developed and deployed responsibly, minimizing harm and bias.
key_concepts: Fairness: Unbiased treatment of all demographics.
Reliability and Safety: Consistent performance without physical or psychological harm.
Privacy and Security: Protecting training data and user information.
Inclusiveness: Designing for accessibility by people of all abilities.
Transparency: Making AI operations and limitations understandable to users.
Accountability: Ensuring humans maintain ultimate responsibility for AI decisions.
important_facts: Transparency often involves explaining the 'black box' of AI.
Accountability requires human oversight, especially in critical sectors like healthcare.
examples: Fairness: Auditing a loan approval model for racial bias.
Inclusiveness: Adding text-to-speech for visually impaired users.
memory_hooks: FRPI-TA (Fairness, Reliability, Privacy, Inclusiveness, Transparency, Accountability).

notes.summary: Machine learning techniques define the approach an algorithm takes to learn from data and make predictions.
key_concepts: Regression: Predicts continuous numerical values (e.g., price, temperature).
Classification: Categorizes data into discrete classes (e.g., spam/not spam).
Clustering: Groups similar unlabeled data points (unsupervised learning).
Deep Learning: Uses multi-layered neural networks for complex patterns.
Transformer Architecture: A neural network design using self-attention, foundational for LLMs.
important_facts: Regression and classification are supervised (require labeled data).
Clustering is unsupervised (works with unlabeled data).
examples: Regression: Predicting a car's top speed.
Clustering: Grouping customers by shopping behavior.
memory_hooks: Regression = Rates/Numbers. Classification = Categories. Clustering = Clumps (unlabeled).

notes.summary: Understanding the fundamental components of machine learning datasets and evaluation is critical for training reliable models.
key_concepts: Features: The input variables or attributes used to make a prediction.
Labels: The target output variable the model is trying to predict.
Training Data: The dataset used to teach the model.
Validation Data: A holdout dataset used to test the model's performance on unseen data.
important_facts: Testing a model on its training data leads to overfitting, where the model memorizes the data but fails in the real world.
examples: In predicting house prices, square footage and zip code are features; the final price is the label.
memory_hooks: Features = Facts you know. Label = What you're Looking for.

notes.summary: Azure Machine Learning provides the cloud infrastructure to manage the entire end-to-end ML lifecycle.
key_concepts: Workspace: The top-level resource/container for all ML assets.
Compute Instance: A managed cloud workstation for developers (Jupyter).
Compute Cluster: Scalable multi-node infrastructure for heavy training jobs.
Endpoints: The deployment interface providing a REST API for real-time model consumption.
important_facts: Compute clusters can scale down to zero nodes to save costs.
Endpoints are required to integrate a trained model into a live application.
examples: Deploying a fraud detection model to an endpoint so the banking app can check transactions in milliseconds.
memory_hooks: Instance = Individual workstation. Cluster = Crowd of VMs for training. Endpoint = End of the line (deployment).

notes.topic_title: Identify common types of computer vision solutions
notes.summary: Computer vision involves extracting meaning and data from visual inputs like images and videos.
key_concepts: Image Classification: Assigning an overall category or tag to an entire image.
Object Detection: Locating specific items within an image and returning bounding box coordinates.
Optical Character Recognition (OCR): Extracting printed or handwritten text from visual media.
Facial Detection: Locating human faces in an image.
Semantic Segmentation: Classifying images at the pixel level to draw precise irregular boundaries.
important_facts: Classification describes the whole image; Object Detection describes where things are in the image.
examples: OCR: Scanning a handwritten sticky note into Microsoft Word.
Object Detection: Drawing a box around every pedestrian in a dashcam video.
memory_hooks: Classification = What is this? Object Detection = Where are they?

notes.topic_title: Identify Azure tools and services for computer vision tasks
notes.summary: Azure offers pre-built and customizable services to integrate vision capabilities into applications.
key_concepts: Azure AI Vision: A comprehensive service for image analysis, OCR, spatial analysis, and generating captions.
Custom Vision: A feature of AI Vision allowing users to train models on their own proprietary images.
Azure AI Face: A specialized service for detecting, verifying, and analyzing human faces.
important_facts: Use Azure AI Vision for general image tasks. Use Azure AI Face specifically for identity verification and advanced facial landmarks.
examples: Using Azure AI Vision Spatial Analysis to count the number of people in a retail store queue.
memory_hooks: Vision sees the world; Face focuses on the people.

notes.topic_title: Identify features of common NLP workload scenarios
notes.summary: Natural Language Processing (NLP) allows AI to understand, analyze, and generate human language.
key_concepts: Sentiment Analysis: Scoring text as positive, negative, or neutral.
Entity Recognition (NER): Categorizing specific nouns like people, places, dates, or PII.
Key Phrase Extraction: Identifying the main concepts as a list of terms.
Text Summarization: Condensing text into a cohesive narrative summary.
Translation: Converting text or speech between languages.
important_facts: NER categorizes entities; Key Phrase Extraction just lists important concepts.
Summarization differs from extraction by generating cohesive text.
examples: Sentiment Analysis: Flagging an angry email from a customer.
Entity Recognition: Highlighting 'Microsoft' as an Organization.
memory_hooks: Sentiment = Feelings. Entity = Nouns. Key Phrases = Bullet points.

notes.topic_title: Identify Azure tools and services for NLP workloads
notes.summary: Azure provides two primary services for handling NLP: Azure AI Language (for text) and Azure AI Speech (for audio).
key_concepts: Azure AI Language: Unifies text processing (Sentiment, NER, Question Answering, CLU, PII).
Conversational Language Understanding (CLU): Maps text to intents and parameters (replaces LUIS).
Azure AI Speech: Handles audio processing (Speech-to-text, Text-to-speech, Translation).
important_facts: LUIS and QnA Maker are retired; they are now capabilities within Azure AI Language.
PII detection is a crucial part of Azure AI Language for data privacy.
examples: Using Azure AI Language Question Answering to build an FAQ chatbot.
memory_hooks: Language reads and understands. Speech listens and talks.

notes.topic_title: Identify features of generative AI solutions
notes.summary: Generative AI focuses on creating novel content (text, images, code) using large foundation models.
key_concepts: Prompt Engineering: The process of designing and refining input text to get optimal model outputs.
Embeddings: Converting text into high-dimensional numerical vectors for semantic search.
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation): Grounding a model's answers in private, retrieved documents to prevent hallucinations.
System Messages: Setting the foundational instructions and persona for a chat model.
Few-shot prompting: Providing examples in the prompt to teach the model how to respond.
important_facts: RAG is the primary architecture for chatting with your own data without fine-tuning.
Embeddings enable AI to understand the 'meaning' of words mathematically.
examples: Using RAG to let an employee query a company handbook.
Using few-shot prompting to teach a model to format outputs as JSON.
memory_hooks: RAG = Retrieve (Search), Augment (Add to prompt), Generate (Answer).

notes.topic_title: Identify generative AI services and capabilities in Microsoft Azure
notes.summary: Azure provides secure, enterprise-grade tools to develop and deploy generative AI applications.
key_concepts: Azure OpenAI Service: Securely hosts OpenAI models (GPT-4o, DALL-E 3) within Azure.
Azure AI Foundry: The comprehensive portal for exploring models, evaluating prompts, and building AI apps.
Model Catalog: A centralized repository in Foundry hosting thousands of models (OpenAI, Meta, etc.).
Azure AI Content Safety: Built-in moderation to block harmful prompts and completions.
important_facts: Azure OpenAI guarantees that customer data is NEVER used to train public foundation models.
GPT-4o is multimodal (handles text and images), while DALL-E 3 generates images.
examples: Using Azure AI Foundry to compare the performance of GPT-4o against a Meta Llama model.
memory_hooks: Foundry = The factory where you build AI apps. Catalog = The store where you pick your model.
